query: What demand charges apply to commercial customers with demand >500 kW in California, within PG&E's territory, based on the OpenEI tariffs data? Answer should contain service class name and demand charge for at least 2 classes
final_answer:
Based on the OpenEI IURDB tariff data for **Pacific Gas & Electric Co** (EIA ID 14328), here are the commercial demand charges applicable to customers with demand **>500 kW**. The relevant schedule is **E-19 (Medium General Demand TOU)**, which applies to commercial customers with peak demand between **500 and 999 kW**. Below are at least two service classes with their demand charges:

---

### 1. E-19 Medium General Demand TOU (Secondary)
- **Voltage Category:** Secondary
- **Demand Range:** 500–999 kW
- **Flat (Facilities) Demand Charge:** **$42.10/kW** (applies during summer months: May–October)
- **Time-of-Use Demand Charges (by tier):**
  - Tier 1: $0.00/kW
  - Tier 2: $0.00/kW
  - Tier 3 (Part-Peak): **$16.44/kW**
  - Tier 4 (Peak): **$21.59/kW**
- **Source:** [OpenEI IURDB – E-19 Secondary](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/69de7e4cd600f18ec30600ac) | [PG&E Tariff PDF](https://www.pge.com/tariffs/assets/pdf/tariffbook/ELEC_SCHEDS_E-19.pdf)

---

### 2. E-19 Medium General Demand TOU (Primary)
- **Voltage Category:** Primary
- **Demand Range:** 500–999 kW
- **Flat (Facilities) Demand Charge:** **$33.10/kW** (applies during summer months: May–October)
- **Time-of-Use Demand Charges (by tier):**
  - Tier 1: $0.00/kW
  - Tier 2: $0.00/kW
  - Tier 3 (Part-Peak): **$14.86/kW**
  - Tier 4 (Peak): **$18.71/kW**
- **Source:** [OpenEI IURDB – E-19 Primary](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/69de7c756d1ebb0263094f0d) | [PG&E Tariff PDF](https://www.pge.com/tariffs/assets/pdf/tariffbook/ELEC_SCHEDS_E-19.pdf)

---

### 3. E-19 Medium General Demand TOU (Transmission)
- **Voltage Category:** Transmission
- **Demand Range:** 500–999 kW
- **Flat Demand Charge:** **$16.47/kW** (year-round)
- **Time-of-Use Demand Charge:** $0.00/kW (no TOU demand component)
- **Source:** [OpenEI IURDB – E-19 Transmission](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/69de7d02edea9919e60bad8e) | [PG&E Tariff PDF](https://www.pge.com/tariffs/assets/pdf/tariffbook/ELEC_SCHEDS_E-19.pdf)

---

### Summary Table

| Service Class | Voltage | Flat Demand Charge ($/kW) | Peak TOU Demand Charge ($/kW) | Part-Peak TOU Demand ($/kW) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| E-19 TOU (Secondary) | Secondary | $42.10 (summer) | $21.59 | $16.44 |
| E-19 TOU (Primary) | Primary | $33.10 (summer) | $18.71 | $14.86 |
| E-19 TOU (Transmission) | Transmission | $16.47 (year-round) | $0.00 | $0.00 |

**Key takeaways:**
- The **flat demand charge** (also called the "facilities charge") is a per-kW charge based on the customer's maximum demand, and it varies significantly by voltage level — secondary voltage customers pay the highest flat demand rate ($42.10/kW), while transmission-level customers pay the lowest ($16.47/kW).
- The **TOU demand charges** apply on top of the flat demand charge and are tiered by time-of-use period (peak, part-peak, off-peak). Secondary and primary voltage customers face peak TOU demand charges of $21.59/kW and $18.71/kW respectively, while transmission-level customers have no TOU demand component.

All data sourced from the **OpenEI IURDB** (Utility Rate Database), queried via the `get_utility_tariffs` tool for PG&E (EIA ID 14328), Commercial sector, active and approved tariffs.
